rag_rules: Insert an AGREE annex only in the owner scope that matches the generated content kind: type-level contract content belongs to a component type, while implementation-level bindings belong to a component implementation.
Preserve the source AADL structure as authoritative: keep existing component names, ports, subcomponents, connections, and properties unchanged while adding the annex.
generic_rules: Attach new annex text to the intended owning declaration instead of rewriting unrelated declarations.
Keep surrounding architecture text intact; if implementation-scoped content depends on declared elements, place the annex after those declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the standard wrapper for each inserted block: annex agree {** ... **};
anti_patterns: Do not place type-level contract text in an implementation scope or implementation-only bindings in a type scope.
Do not modify ports, connections, subcomponents, or property associations just to make the annex fit.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE expression typing or helper-declaration type repair inside clauses
Numeric range comparisons for nonnumeric or opaque data
Using AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were in-scope AGREE variables
